items.description: Paper defines Ea = -ln(c0) but data shows Ea = 9.465 with c0 = 0.10565, which is mathematically inconsistent. Either the formula is wrong or the computation is different.
items.action: Inspect analysis_h3.py and analysis_h2.py to determine actual Ea computation. Correct either the formula in the paper or the data.

items.description: Paper cites [1] through [11] but has no References section.
items.action: Add complete References section with full bibliographic entries for all citations. Verify 'Li 2026' exists.

items.issue: H3 source data mislabeled as CONFIRMED
items.description: analysis_h3.json labels H3 as CONFIRMED based on threshold test alone, ignoring AUC=0.436 and Spearman=-0.063.
items.action: Update analysis_h3.json and analysis_h3_summary.md to reflect FALSIFIED status. Update evaluation logic to require AUC > 0.5.

items.issue: Table 8 lacks quantitative error classification data
items.description: Section 6.2 claims execution errors 'dominate' low-Ea failures but provides no counts or proportions.
items.action: Either perform quantitative error classification on low-Ea failures and add Count/Proportion columns, or soften claim to 'we hypothesize'.

items.description: Optimal threshold 9.9999999999 is optimized on evaluation data. The 25pp 'error floor' is an optimistic upper bound.
items.action: Clarify in Section 6.3 that 25pp is an upper bound from post-hoc optimization. Consider cross-validated threshold.

items.issue: Sample size too small for robust conclusions
items.description: n=50 problems with only 2 at Level 1. No confidence intervals reported for key statistics.
items.action: Add bootstrap confidence intervals for R2, Spearman, and AUC. Acknowledge small sample prominently.

items.issue: Per-problem fit failure undermines H1 claim
items.description: Median R2=0.000, 80% fit failure, only 8/50 prefer exponential by AICc. Aggregate R2=0.924 may be averaging artifact.
items.action: Add bootstrap confidence intervals for aggregate R2. Discuss whether exponential form is mechanism or curve-fitting artifact.

items.issue: Novelty claim is narrow and fragile
items.description: Novelty rests entirely on H3 negative result. ACAR (2026) has related 'agreement-but-wrong' finding.
items.action: Strengthen differentiation from ACAR by contrasting consistency-based vs variance-based routing. Cite ACAR properly.

items.description: All experiments use Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct. Broad claims about consistency-based routing exceed evidence.
items.action: Either run same experiment on second model, or temper claims to 'on Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct'.

items.description: H5 (consistency-Ea vs saturation-k0) is falsified (Spearman=-0.219, p=0.54) but only briefly mentioned.
items.action: Expand discussion of H5 in Section 6. Explain why the two measures diverge and what this means for theory.

items.description: 'Arrhenius kinetics' vs 'Arrhenius-like kinetics'. Table 7 status capitalization inconsistent.
items.action: Standardize to glossary-preferred terms. Fix Table 7 formatting.

items.description: P_infinity * k0 conflates ceiling and rate parameters.
items.action: Remove or rephrase sentence to avoid conflating parameters.

items.issue: 'Prove' is too strong for statistical evidence

items.description: Outline plans appendices but none are present.
items.action: Add at minimum full problem data table and extraction audit.

items.description: All 13 Level 5 problems have Ea ~10.0 with sigma ~1.9e-6. Suggests computational artifact.
items.action: Investigate and explain artifact, or report raw c0 values.
